steps.title: Substitute into the denominator
steps.content: The function is $f(x)=\dfrac{1}{6x}$. When $x=3$, the denominator is 6 times 3, not 6 plus 3.

steps.title: Simplify the fraction
steps.content:
$$f(3)=\dfrac{1}{6(3)}=\dfrac{1}{18}$$
The expression equals one over the product 18, so D is correct. Choices with larger fractions come from using a smaller denominator or treating $6x$ as something other than multiplication.
